Container.Name: coffee-tracker
Container.Overview: Self-hosted coffee catalog: rate coffees over time, snap a bag photo to auto-fill the form (OCR), multi-user. Serves the Angular PWA and the API same-origin on port 8080. Place behind a reverse proxy that terminates HTTPS.
Container.Requires: Set a strong Jwt__Key (e.g. `openssl rand -base64 48`) — the app refuses to start without one. To create the first account, set REGISTRATION_ENABLED=true, register (the first user to sign up becomes admin), then set it back to false. Put the container behind a TLS-terminating reverse proxy and set ForwardedHeaders__KnownProxies to the proxy IP.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: HTTP port the app listens on. Map your reverse proxy to this.

Config.Name: Config (SQLite DB)
Config.Description: Holds coffee.db. Back this up before updating the image.

Config.Name: Photos
Config.Description: Uploaded coffee photos. Back this up before updating.

Config.Name: JWT Signing Key
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Long random secret for signing auth tokens. The app refuses to start without a strong value. Generate e.g. with: openssl rand -base64 48

Config.Name: Registration Enabled
Config.Description: When false (default) new signups are blocked — safe for a public/shared instance. Set true to allow registration; the first user to register becomes admin.

Config.Name: Known Proxies
Config.Description: Comma-separated IPs of your reverse proxy (e.g. 172.17.0.1). Lets the app trust X-Forwarded-* headers.
